title: Kalesar National Park
tagline: Kalesar National Park — wildlife reserve of Haryana

overview.short: Kalesar National Park (13,000 acres (53 km2)) and adjacent Kalesar Wildlife Sanctuary (13,209 acres (53.45 km2) are protected areas in Kalesar of Yamunanagar district of Haryana st
overview.description: Kalesar National Park (13,000 acres (53 km2)) and adjacent Kalesar Wildlife Sanctuary (13,209 acres (53.45 km2) are protected areas in Kalesar of Yamunanagar district of Haryana state in India, 46 kilometres from Yamunanagar city, 122 kilometres (76 mi) from Chandigarh. Kalesar National Park was established in 2003. Kalesar National Park and Kalesar Wildlife Sanctuary are contiguous to Simbalbara National Park in Himachal Pradesh and Rajaji National Park in Uttarakhand. Kalesar is a popular destination for leopards, panthers, elephants, red jungle fowl and bird-watching.

topPlaces.name: Hathni Kund
topPlaces.description: Hathni Kund is a location of origin of Western Yamuna Canal in the Indian state of Haryana, built on a raised plinth overlooking the Yamuna River. The river flows into the plains of Haryana after crossing over the last lap of the Himalayan foothills at Paonta Sahib.

topPlaces.name: Hathni Kund Barrage
topPlaces.description: The Hathni Kund is a concrete barrage located on the Yamuna River in Yamuna Nagar district of Haryana state, India. It was constructed between October 1996 and June 1999 for the purpose of irrigation.

topPlaces.name: Khizri
topPlaces.description: Khizri is a village/hamlet located in Chhachhrauli tehsil of Yamunanagar district in Haryana, India. It is 22 kilometres (14 mi) from the sub-district headquarters at Chhachhrauli and 32 kilometres (20 mi) from the district headquarters at Yamunanagar.

topPlaces.name: Ch. Devi Lal Herbal Nature Park
topPlaces.description: The Ch. Devi Lal Rudraksh Vatika Herbal Nature Park, in short Rudraksh Vatika, is a 184-acre forested wildlife area, wetland and herbal park for the conservation of biodiversity of over 400 endangered ayurvedic medicinal herbs in Shivalik foothills of Himalayas.

image.alt: Ch. Devi Lal Herbal Nature Park

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalesar National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalesar National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalesar National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Dehradun Railway Station (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalesar National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalesar National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalesar National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Kalesar National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalesar National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hathni Kund, Hathni Kund Barrage, Khizri, Ch. Devi Lal Herbal Nature Park.
